chief_complaint: A 75-year-old male patient presents with shortness of breath and chest pain 10 days after a myocardial infarction.
history: The patient had a history of hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and smoking. He underwent a percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) 10 days ago. He has been experiencing shortness of breath and chest pain since then. He has been on aspirin, atorvastatin, and metoprolol.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 100 bpm, RR 24/min. Physical examination reveals a new holosystolic murmur best heard at the left lower sternal border.
labs: ECG: new Q-waves in leads V2-V5. Troponin: elevated. Echocardiogram: ventricular septal defect with a shunt.
correct_diagnosis: Ventricular Septal Rupture Secondary to Late-Presenting Myocardial Infarction
differentials: Ventricular Septal Rupture Secondary to Late-Presenting Myocardial Infarction
Free-Wall Rupture Secondary to Myocardial Infarction
Papillary Muscle Rupture Secondary to Myocardial Infarction
correct_ranking: Ventricular Septal Rupture Secondary to Late-Presenting Myocardial Infarction
Free-Wall Rupture Secondary to Myocardial Infarction
Papillary Muscle Rupture Secondary to Myocardial Infarction
